metaDescription: Cricket extermination in NYC. Stop the chirping and the damage. Licensed exterminators. Free inspection. Same-day available. Call now.
h1: Cricket Control in NYC — Stop the Chirping and Protect Your Belongings
heroParagraph: House crickets and camel crickets are the two species most commonly found inside New York City buildings. House crickets are the familiar chirping insect — the chirping sound you hear at night is male crickets attracting mates, and a single cricket inside a bedroom is genuinely disruptive to sleep. Camel crickets (also called cave crickets or spider crickets) are a different matter: they're large, humpbacked, wingless, and silent — most people confuse them with large spiders when they first encounter one jumping across a basement floor. They're harmless but abundant in basements, crawl spaces, and damp storage areas.
bodyParagraphs: Cricket infestations in NYC buildings occur when crickets enter seeking warmth and shelter in late summer and fall as temperatures drop. They're attracted to exterior lighting (which brings flying insects that crickets feed on, and also draws crickets directly) and enter through foundation gaps, under door thresholds, and through any ground-level opening. Basements and ground floors are most affected. Crickets also breed indoors in humid conditions — a camel cricket population in a damp basement can establish itself year-round without any outdoor connection.

Unlike cockroaches, crickets don't spread disease and don't cause structural damage. The issues are noise (house crickets), psychological stress (camel crickets jumping unexpectedly), and, in large numbers, feeding damage to fabric, paper, and stored food. Treatment is straightforward but should include entry point sealing to prevent reinfestation from the abundant outdoor cricket population each fall.
signs: Chirping sounds at night from inside the basement or walls — male house crickets
Large, humpbacked, gray-brown crickets in basement or storage areas — camel crickets
Cricket damage on fabric, paper, or stored food items — irregular holes and chew marks
Cricket frass (cylindrical black droppings) near harborage areas
Cricket sightings on exterior foundation wall at night, drawn to exterior lighting
Increased indoor cricket activity in September–October as outdoor temperatures drop
treatmentApproach: Cricket treatment uses granular bait along the perimeter exterior to intercept crickets before entry, residual liquid spray at the foundation perimeter and entry points, and crack-and-crevice treatment in basement and ground-floor areas. Interior treatments target basement perimeter, under appliances, and storage areas where camel crickets concentrate. Glue board monitoring confirms elimination. Entry point sealing at threshold gaps and foundation openings prevents fall reinfestation. Treatment is most effective when timed in late August before the September–October migration indoors begins.

faqs.q: The chirping is driving me crazy — can you treat tonight?
faqs.a: We offer same-day and emergency appointments. A single house cricket inside living space can be removed on first inspection. If you have a larger population, full treatment is more effective — we'll advise based on what the inspection finds.

No. Camel crickets look alarming due to their size and jumping behavior but don't bite, don't chirp, and don't carry disease. They're primarily a nuisance in basements and damp storage areas. Large populations can cause minor fabric damage in storage areas.

faqs.q: How much does cricket control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Cricket treatment starts at $350–$550 for a standard home or apartment. Written estimate provided before starting.

faqs.q: Are crickets actually harmful or just annoying?
faqs.a: Primarily annoying — but crickets can cause real damage in sufficient numbers. They chew on fabrics (particularly wool, cotton, and silk), paper, rubber, and even leather. In large infestations, stored clothing, curtains, and upholstered furniture are all at risk. In commercial settings, including restaurants and warehouses, significant product damage is possible. The non-stop chirping also disrupts sleep, which becomes a quality-of-life issue quickly.

faqs.q: Why do crickets keep coming back every year?
faqs.a: Crickets overwinter as eggs in soil. Even after adult crickets die off in cold weather, egg masses remain viable and hatch the following season. Without exterior barrier treatment before the spring hatch, the cycle repeats annually. Properties near parks, green spaces, or construction sites in NYC are especially prone to recurring cricket pressure due to the abundance of breeding habitat nearby.

faqs.q: Do I need to treat the outside of my building to stop crickets?
faqs.a: For lasting results, yes. Interior treatments address the current population, but crickets are constantly migrating indoors from outside. Exterior perimeter treatment — particularly around foundation gaps, door thresholds, utility penetrations, and window wells — is the most effective way to prevent re-entry. We treat both interior and exterior as part of our comprehensive cricket control approach.
